publicationstmt.publisher: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday May 4, 2026 at 22:00

corpname.part: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

did.abstract: The collection includes materials created and collected by staff and members of the Connecticut State Medical Society (CSMS), founded in 1792. Materials document the history and activities of the organization, policies regarding the practice of medicine in Connecticut, and efforts to effect legislative policy and regulations regarding the practice of medicine at the state and national levels of government. Materials include Council agendas, minutes, and supporting documents; House of Delegates annual meeting handbooks, proceedings, and planning files; publications produced by CSMS, office files and reports created by several staff members; photographs of events, CSMS presidents, and spaces; scrapbooks; ephemera created and collected by CSMS; posters; microfilm; and miscellany.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of the Connecticut State Medical Society, 2020.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is organized in 9 series: Series I. Council, 1924-2007. Series II: House of Delegates, 1905-2009. Series III: Office files, 1918-2013. Series IV: Publications, 1959-2013. Series V: Ledgers, 1866-1952. Series VI: Scrapbooks, 1946-1959. Series VII: Photographs, 1792-1971. Series VIII. Ephemera and miscellany, 1785-2003. Series IX. Microfilm, 1792-1991. (Dates for Series VII. Photographs and Series IX. Microfilm reflect dates of original content, not dates of creation.)

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The Connecticut State Medical Society (CSMS) is one of the oldest extant medical associations in the United States. Founded in 1792 as the Connecticut Medical Society, the organization is a federation of eight component county medical associations (Fairfield, Hartford, Litchfield, Middlesex, New Haven, New London, Tolland, and Windham) which is in turn part of a federation of state medical societies that are constituent entities of the American Medical Association. The current stated mission of the CSMS is to be the voice of Connecticut physicians; to lead physicians in advocacy; to promote the profession of medicine; to improve the quality of care; and to safeguard the health of our patients.

The history of the Connecticut State Medical Society is closely entwined with the history of the practice of medicine and medical education in the state of Connecticut. Creation of the CSMS was initiated by members of the New Haven Medical Society, many of whom were associated with Yale College. After a multi-year campaign to create a state-wide medical society, the General Assembly of the State of Connecticut passed the Act of Incorporation of the Connecticut Medical Society in 1792. The stated purposes of the Society were the improving of medical practice, medical education, and friendly relations among physicians. The terms of the charter included the formation of eight county medical associations, whose membership would comprise membership of the state medical society, and who would elect representatives to attend state meetings. The first president of CSMS and one third of the charter members were graduates of Yale College.

In its first year, the CSMS set up processes for aspiring physicians to take medical exams, for the granting of licenses to practice medicine in the state of Connecticut, and for the discipline of members who violated CSMS rules of conduct and banned the prescription of secret proprietary and patent medicines.

In 1807 Timothy Dwight, President of Yale College, asked the CSMS to help establish a medical school at Yale. In 1810 the Connecticut Legislature approved the joint endeavor to form the Medical Institution at Yale College, the precursor to what is now known as the Yale School of Medicine. The CSMS helped select faculty and examined candidates for medical degrees and each of the County Medical Associations were allowed to name a student from their county to attend the school for free. This arrangement ended in 1885 when the articles of union between the Society and the College were set aside and Yale assumed full control of the school.

CSMS began advocating for public care for people suffering from mental illness in 1812, forming a committee "to collect information concerning the number of lunatics in the State, their whereabouts and their care." In 1821 CSMS launched a campaign to establish the Connecticut Retreat for the Insane (later known as the Hartford Retreat, now called the Institute of Living) the first institution for treatment of mental illness in Connecticut. A donation from CSMS helped fund the hospital, which opened in 1824. CSMS also played a large role in the creation of the Connecticut Hospital for the Insane (now known as Connecticut Valley Hospital) which opened in Middletown in 1867.

The CSMS also played a central role in the founding of Yale-New Haven Hospital. In 1825 the Society voted to adopt the recommendation of an internal committee that a general hospital that would serve the interests of the Medical Institution of Yale College be created. The General Assembly of the State of Connecticut passed an act incorporating the General Hospital Society of Connecticut the following year with a charter stipulating the President and Fellows of the Connecticut Medical Society should be ex-officio members. CSMS helped pay for the hospital by designating five dollars from the fee charged to each recipient of an M.D. degree in the state of Connecticut to the project fund. In 1828 the Society appointed a committee to forward the establishment of a general hospital and in 1829 issued a circular recommending the institution to the people of Connecticut. The State Hospital (later known as New Haven Hospital, then Grace-New Haven Hospital, then Yale-New Haven Hospital) opened in 1833 on seven and a half acres of land bordered by Cedar Street and Howard, Davenport, and Congress avenues.

Barker, Creighton, The first seventy-five years of the Connecticut State Medical Society, An address presented at the Sesquicentennial Celebration of The Connecticut State Medical Society at Middletown, June 4, 1942. https://archive.org/details/connecticutmedic5616conn/page/80/mode/2up

Thoms, Herbert, The Medical Institution of Yale College and the Connecticut State Medical Society, Connecticut Medicine , September 1960. https://archive.org/details/connecticutmedic24unse/page/546/mode/2up

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Connecticut State Medical Society Records. Historical Library, Harvey Cushing/John Hay Whitney Medical Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The collection includes materials created and collected by staff and members of the Connecticut State Medical Society (CSMS), founded in 1792. Materials document the history and activities of the organization, policies regarding the practice of medicine in Connecticut, and efforts to effect legislative policy and regulations regarding the practice of medicine at the state and national levels of government.

Series I. Council, 1924-2007 includes materials generated by the functions of the CSMS Council, which creates legislative and regulatory policy for the organization. The Council chair serves on the CSMS Board of Directors. The series includes typescript Council minutes in bound volumes; binders of Council minutes with House of Delegates summaries; binders of auxiliary Council minutes including supporting documents such as correspondence, clippings, memoranda, and ephemera; and office files.

Series II. House of Delegates, 1905-2009, includes materials related to annual and semi-annual meetings of the CSMS House of Delegates. Members of the House of Delegates are elected by Connecticut county medical associations and meetings are open to all CSMS members. The series includes typescript proceedings in bound volumes and folders and Annual and Semi-annual Meeting handbooks, ephemera, and planning files.

Series III. Office files, 1918-2013, includes materials created by numerous CSMS officials and staff. Materials include reports and correspondence sent from Connecticut county medical societies to CSMS, 1918-1937; files created by Executive Director Creighton Barker, 1937-1951, including state medical examiner correspondence and materials related to governor commissions on the prevention and care of sickness, care of veterans, and treatment of people with disabilities; files regarding the Connecticut Medical Service, the precursor to Blue Cross of Connecticut, 1945-1994; general office files, 1960-2016; files created by Robert Brunell, Director of Scientific Affairs, including materials regarding medical quackery, 1962-2010; Tim Norbeck, Executive Director speeches, testimonies, and writings, 1983-2006; files created by Ken Ferrucci, Vice President of Public Policy and Government Affairs and Acting Executive Director, including materials regarding medical malpractice and liability, 1999-2004; and communications and media files, 2006-2013.

Series IV. Publications, 1959–2013 contains copies of publications created by CSMS including Action , Connecticut M. D. , Legislative Bulletins and Alerts, CSMS Newsletter , Physician Health Matters , CSMS Political Roundup ; Resident Watch , and Sports Med . The series also includes minutes from the Connecticut Medicine Editorial Board.

Series V. Ledgers, 1866–1952 includes ten ledgers and account books created by CSMS to track income and expenses including membership dues, Connecticut Medical Board exam fees, building funds, and the CSMS Women's Auxiliary.

Series VI. Scrapbooks, 1946–1959 includes nine scrapbooks containing clippings from Connecticut newspapers and news services. Topics of clippings include CSMS, health care in Connecticut, Creighton Barker "Connecticut Pharmacist" columns, emergency call services, Connecticut medical schools and medical students, CSMS and Yale School of Medicine Clinical Congress events, "Your Health" columns, and the Connecticut Medical Service.

Series VII. Photographs, 1792–1971 includes a series of black and white prints of CSMS presidents from 1792-1980, photographs of CSMS events and buildings, photograph portraits of CSMS presidents and associates, and negatives of photographs used to produce the CSMS presidents print series.

Series VIII. Ephemera and miscellany, 1785–2003 includes ephemera from Clinical Congress events presented by CSMS and the Yale School of Medicine; early Connecticut newspapers with announcements regarding CSMS; pamphlets and brochures created by CSMS and other medical societies or groups; posters created for CSMS campaigns regarding medical insurance; and miscellaneous items including a painted portrait of physician Samuel Perry (1786-1821), a framed letter to William Osler, CSMS advertising campaign printing blocks, an embroidered AMA delegate badge, and a VHS tape recording with content regarding a medical malpractice rally.
Series IX. Microfilm, 1792–1991 includes 127 reels of microfilm containing copies of CSMS proceeding, 1792-1991; journals, 1936-1990; Council minutes, 1924-1986; and auxiliary minutes, 1954-1989.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

corpname.part: Blue Cross & Blue Shield of Connecticut

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Some materials in this series were removed from original enclosures and housed in archival folders and boxes for preservation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes materials from medical liability reform campaigns, including advertisements, information packets, correspondence, press coverage, events, and congressional testimony.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Front part of ledger contains an alphabetical list of members and chapters with amounts paid, the remainder of ledger includes sections with entries for dues paid, withdrawals, and account balances. Enclosures including correspondence, reports and loose pages were removed and housed separately for preservation.

did.unittitle: Connecticut State Medical Society ledger enclosures

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Material removed from 1866-1931 ledger, includes correspondence, reports, and loose ledger pages.

did.unittitle: Connecticut State Medical Examining Board account book

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Entries note payment of fees for admittance to the Connecticut Medical Board exam.

did.unittitle: Connecticut State Medical Examining Board account book

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Entries note payment of fees for admittance to the Connecticut Medical Board exam.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Entries record members' payment of dues.

did.unittitle: Connecticut State Medical Society building funds ledger

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Entries record pledges to the Connecticut State Medical Society Building Committee 1944-1951 and follow up drive for Council room addition 1956-1958. Enclosed correspondence was removed and housed separately.

did.unittitle: Connecticut State Medical Society Women's Auxiliary ledger

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Entries record members' payment of dues.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Entries record members' payment of dues.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Red cover. Scrapbook contains clippings from a column written by Creighton Barker on a variety of topics including the Connecticut Medical Service, socialized medicine, and a new emergency medical call plan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Very large scrapbook with a black cover. Contains clippings from Connecticut newspapers on topics related to medicine.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dark blue cover with gold stripe. Contains clippings from Connecticut newspapers on topics related to medicine including insurance, hypnosis, general medical information, and pending legislation.

did.unittitle: Journal of the American Medical Association clippings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dark blue cover with gold border. Scrapbook contains short clippings from the Journal of the American Medical Association regarding medicine in Connecticut. Topics include lectures, conferences, grant awards, new construction, and research.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This item is too fragile for research use, it is restricted pending digitization.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dark green cover. Scrapbook contains newspaper clippings regarding a new emergency medical call service in Connecticut.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Teal cover. Scrapbook contains newspaper clippings regarding the University of Connecticut's Medical School, Yale Medical School, and other information about medical schools in Connecticut.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brown cover. Scrapbook contains clippings from Connecticut newspapers regarding Clinical Congress events presented by the Connecticut State Medical Society and the Yale School of Medicine.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Maroon cover. Scrapbook contains newspaper clippings from "Your Health" advice column published in Connecticut newspapers.

did.unittitle: Annual meeting 1958 Connecticut Medical Service controversy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brown cover. Scrapbook contains newspaper clippings regarding the conflict between the Connecticut State Medical Society and the Connecticut Medical Service over representation on the Connecticut Medical Service Board of Directors.

did.unittitle: Prints of Connecticut State Medical Society presidents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black and white prints of engravings, paintings, and photographic portraits of CSMS presidents, includes names and dates of service. Original photographs used as sources of some images may be found in photograph portraits, A-Z.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hubbard, Leverett, 1792-1794 (no image); Munson, Eneas, 1794-1801; Potter, James, 1801-1803 (no image); Mosley, Thomas, 1803-1804 (no image); West, Jeremiah, 1804-1807 (no image); Watrous, John R., 1807-1812; Cogswell, Mason F., 1812-1822; Buel, William, 1832-1834 (no image); Fuller, Silas, 1837-1841; Middlebrook, Elijah, 1841-1843

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ticknor, Luther, 1843-1846 (no image); Welch, Archibald, 1846-1849; Sumner, George, 1849-1851; Blakeman, Rufus, 1851-1853; Warner, Richard, 1853-1854; Cogswell, William H., 1854-1856; Catlin, Benjamin H., 1856-1858; Woodward, Ashbel, 1858-1861; Beckwith, Josiah G., 1861-1863; Hunt, Ebenezer K., 1863-1864

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ives, Nathan B., 1865-1866 (no image); Porter, Isaac, 1866-1867; Woodward, Charles, 1867-1868; Beresford, Samuel B., 1868-1869; Bronson, Henry, 1869-1870; Sumner, Charles F., 1870-1871; Russell, Gurdon W., 1871-1872; Buel, Henry W., 1872-1873

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hutchinson, Ira, 1873-1874; Holbrook, Lowell, 1874-1875; Jewett, Pliny A., 1875-1876; Barrows, Asbel W., 1876-1877; Hubbard, Robert, 1877-1878; Carleton, Charles M., 1878-1879 (no photograph); Goodrich, Alfred R., 1879-1880; Platt, Gideon L., 1880-1881; Deming, William, 1881-1882

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brownson, William G., 1882-1883; Nye, Elisha B., 1883-1884; Comings, Benjamin N., 1884-1885; Kinney, Elijah C., 1885-1886; Hills, T. Morton, 1886-1887; Bacon, Francis, 1887-1888; Porter, George L., 1888-1889; Brown, Orlando, 1889-1890

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Storrs, Melancthon, 1890-1891; Lindsley, Charles A., 1891-1892; Newton, Cyrus B., 1892-1893; Edgerton, Francis D., 1893-1894; Braman, Francis N., 1894-1895; Hill, Seth, 1895-1896; Rienzi Robinson, 1896-1897

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Goodwin, Ralph S., 1897-1898; Stearns, Henry P., 1898-1899; Rodman, Charles S, 1899-1900; Almy, Leonard B., 1900-1901; Grannis, John H., 1901-1902; Shelton, Gould A., 1902-1903; St. John, Samuel B., 1903-1904; Carmalt, William H., 1904-1905; Welch, Edward H., 1905

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wordin, Nathaniel E., 1905-1906; Higgins, William L., 1906-1907; McKnight, Everett J., 1907-1908; Overlock, Seldom B., 1908-1909; Gilbert, Samuel D., 1909-1910

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hallock, Frank K., 1910-1911; Stanton, John G., 1911-1912; Bradstreet, E.T., 1912-1913; Brown, D.C., 1913-1914; Smith, Oliver C., 1914-1915; Maher, Stephen J., 1915;

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mailhouse, Max, 1915-1916; Garlick, Samuel M., 1916-1917; Root, Edward K., 1917-1918; Bartlett, Charles J., 1918-1919; Graves, Charles B., 1919-1920

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Blumer, George, 1920-1921; Godfrey, Charles C., 1921-1922; Lyman, David R., 1922-1923; Pratt, Elias, 1923-1924; Ferris, Harry B., 1924-1925

scopecontent.p: White, Robert C., 1925-1926; Wheeler, Frank H., 1926-1927; Sullivan, Daniel, 1927-1928; Smith, Terry, 1928-1929; Verdi, William F., 1929-1930; Graves, Frederick G., 1930-1931; Gildersleeve, Charles C., 1931-1932

scopecontent.p: Lafield, William A., 1932; Shanniss, Trevin (?), 1932; Hanley, John P., 1932-1933; McDonnell, Ralph A., 1933-1934; Steiner, Walter, 1934-1935

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Murdock, Thomas, 1935-1936; Patterson, Daniel, 1936-1937; Turkington, Charles H., 1937-1938; Campbell, Hugh B., 1938-1939; Linde, Joseph I., 1939-1940

scopecontent.p: Whelan, Edward J., 1952-1953; Gildersleeve, George H., 1953-1954; Marvin, H.M., 1954-1955; Stringfield, Oliver L., 1955-1956; Walker, W. Bradford, 1957-1958; Ogden, Ralph T., 1956-1957; Russell, Walter I., 1958-1959; Weise, Ellwood C., Sr., 1959-1960; Gallivan, John N., 1960-1961; Feeney, Thomas M., 1961-1962

scopecontent.p: Deming, Clyde L., 1962-1963; Pitock, Morris P., 1963-1964; Gilman, Ralph L., 1964-1965; Cullen, James R., 1965-1966; Hess, Orvan W., 1966-1967; Bradley, E. Tremain, 1967-1968; Gardner, Norman H., 1968-1969; Martin, Stevens J., 1969-1970; Granoff, Morris A., 1970-1971

scopecontent.p: Weber, Frederick C., Jr., 1971-1972; Grendon, David A., 1972-1973; Cramer, Sidney L., 1973-1974; Root, J. Harold, Jr., 1974-1975; Van Syckle, Guy W., 1975-1976; Spitz, Hilliard, 1976-1977; Friedberg, Isadore H., 1977-1978

scopecontent.p: Freedman, Jerome K., 1978-1979; Kamens, Edward A., 1979-1980

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Most prints in this subseries are 8 x 10 inches, black and white. Many include photographer stamps and hand written notes on reverse side.

did.unittitle: Annual Meeting Proof Sheets and Negatives
Nose Surgery Post-Graduate Course News Release with Photograph
State and County Medical Society Exhibit
Proof Sheets for Publication with CSMS Presidents Portraits
Negatives of Copy Photographs of Correspondence, Miscellaneous

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives of portraits of Connecticut State Medical Society presidents.

did.unittitle: Annual dinner, New Haven Lawn Club, April 20, 1961

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In 1925 the Connecticut State Medical Society began presenting annual events in collaboration with the Yale School of Medicine. These "Clinical Congress" events included lectures, presentations of papers, demonstrations, dinners, and entertainment.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newspapers contain articles and notices regarding the Connecticut State Medical Society.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notice regarding Connecticut State Medical Society meeting and availability of smallpox inoculation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notice regarding the adoption of the Connecticut State Medical Society's bill of incorporation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notice regarding activities of the Connecticut State Medical Society at the 1791 convention.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notice of the Connecticut State Medical Society bylaws.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notice of the activities of the Connecticut State Medical Society at the 1802 annual convention.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes printed materials produced by the Connecticut State Medical Society, collected by staff, and/or donated by members.

title.part: The Medicare Law - P.L. 89-97 An assessment of its professional implications

title.part: Connecticut State Medical Society...medicine's voice in Connecticut

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brochure packet, 1 copy each in English and Spanish.

title.part: 1846-1996 Sesquicentennial celebration of the Hartford Medical Society

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes posters created by the Connecticut State Medical Society.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Connecticut State Medical Society and American Medical Association poster, on foamcore with display stand.

did.unittitle: Four years of college. Four more years of intense medical education...

scopecontent.p: Connecticut State Medical Society poster, from "The M.D. makes the difference" campaign. 3 copies.

did.unittitle: Speak up for the 70,000 people the state wants to forget

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Connecticut State Medical Society poster, on foamcore.

did.unittitle: We won't say no to our patients...but a Medicaid crisis is looming...

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Connecticut State Medical Society poster, formerly adhered to foamcore.

did.unittitle: Which health insurance is right for you?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes materials produced by the Connecticut State Medical Society, collected by staff, and/or donated by members. Several items are related to the history of medicine.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Oil painting on paper, 22.5 x 26.25 cm (8 7/8 x 10 1/4 inches). Handwritten on back of frame: Dr. Samuel Perry, son of Dr. Daniel Perry (father of ? Perry). Handwritten note formerly glued to back of frame: Samuel Perry, M.D. born 1786-died 1821-portrait 1810. Handwritten note partially glued to back of frame: ? Fairfield County (Connecticut) Medical Society.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From the 1871 New Haven City Yearbook. Print was removed from frame for preservation.

did.unittitle: Manuscript letter from James Craig, M.D., to William Osler, April 8, 1905

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: On letterhead from Royal College of Physicians, Ireland, Kildare Street, Dublin. Letter announces Osler was elected Honorary Fellow of the Royal College of Physicians, Ireland. Framed between glass, letter is water damaged and does exhibit presence of mold.

did.unittitle: Smith, Nathan, photograph of a painted portrait

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Was displayed framed with the label: Nathan Smith Club

did.unittitle: New Haven Board of Health proceedings, March 1806

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photostat of page from logbook. Removed from frame for preservation.

did.unittitle: Samuel Gold, diploma and license to practice medicine and surgery

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photostat copies. Original folder was labeled "copies on wall Dr. B's office."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 4 black and white prints with descriptions taped to the reverse and Lederle company stamp. Topics include Vesalius, Ruysch, Egyptian water storage, and surgery in India.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: American Medical Association clip-on badge for jacket breast pocket. Gold embroidery on blue and black velvet background with image of whale and AMA symbol. Text on border: Connecticut State Medical Society Delegation.

did.unittitle: Connecticut Medical Service advertisement printing block

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Created by Anaconda American Brass Company, metal adhered to wood, 7 x 5 1/2 x 7/8 inches. Text: Over 1,350,000 Connecticut Members prepay doctors bills through Connecticut's Blue Shield Plan. Connecticut Medical Service, Inc. 221 Whitney Avenue, New Haven, Connecticut 06509.

did.unittitle: Aetna Life and Casualty advertisement printing block

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Created by Anaconda American Brass Company, metal adhered to wood, 6 7/8 x 4 7/8 x 1 inches. Text: CSMS and Aetna Life and Casualty working together for loss control. This is our greatest objective. The Standard Life Insurance Company, Hartford, Connecticut.

did.unittitle: Medical malpractice insurance doctor's rally, March 26, 2003

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: VHS tape produced by VMS. Includes clips from multiple television channel news reports aired on March 26, 2003. List of clips included in box.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Graham Magnetics Verituf computer data tape. Reel is labeled: User rack, 1600 BPI standard labeled, Connecticut ambulatory care data, J. Hardie, CACS78. Label on reverse: 118371 112.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Graham Magnetics Verituf computer data tape. Reel is labeled: cacs 333333, 1600 standard, Robert Leary, ISS 004, Bin 34. Label on reverse: 77586-26.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 16mm microfilm, titles and alphanumeric codes are copied from original container labels.
